subject: Sociology
title: The Poverty Line
description: Learn The Poverty Line in one minute.

hook.text: The U.S. poverty line was invented in 1963 based on food costs times three. The formula hasn't changed since.

buildup.text: Mollie Orshansky created the threshold: a minimum food budget multiplied by three, adjusted for family size.

discovery.text: In the 1960s, food was a third of expenses. Today, housing and healthcare dominate. The formula is outdated.

twist.text: Millions living just above the line struggle as much as those below it. The line creates a false binary.

climax.text: The Supplemental Poverty Measure accounts for more costs and benefits but still can't capture lived hardship fully.

punchline.text: The poverty line measures something. Just not actual poverty.

quiz.question: What is the main criticism of the U.S. poverty line?
options: It's based on outdated 1960s spending patterns
It's too generous and overestimates poverty
It was never used by the government
